question_title: Will the interest in “patrick warns talarico could win” change between 2026-04-14 and 2026-04-25 according to Google Trends?
summary: The search term "patrick warns talarico could win" spiked to 100 on Apr 12, then rapidly decayed to 38 (Apr 13) and 6 (Apr 14). The baseline was 0 for 17 consecutive days before the spike. By Apr 25, interest will almost certainly be at 0, which is more than 3 below the Apr 14 value of 6, resolving as "Decreases." The main uncertainty is SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement differences — if SerpAPI reports Apr 14 ≤ 3, the outcome flips to "Doesn't change." There's a small chance of a new spike if Patrick makes new warnings about Talarico, but this specific phrase is unlikely to resurface.

factors.description: Post-spike decay pattern: value went 100→38→6 in 2 days, strongly suggesting Apr 25 will be 0. Baseline was 0 for 17 consecutive days before spike.
Current Apr 14 value is 6, above the ±3 threshold floor. For Decreases, Apr 25 just needs to be < 3 (i.e., 0-2). With baseline at 0, this is very likely.
SerpAPI vs pytrends measurement uncertainty: if SerpAPI reports Apr 14 ≤ 3 instead of 6, then Apr 25 = 0 resolves as 'Doesn't change'. SerpAPI can differ by 1-3 points.
Texas Senate race is active but search phrase is very specific to one news cycle. New developments would use different search terms.
Small possibility of new Dan Patrick statements about Talarico generating renewed searches with this exact phrase, causing Apr 25 spike.

reasoning:
The reflection was approved. Let me finalize my forecast.

**Key findings:**

1. **Post-spike decay is the dominant pattern.** The search term spiked to 100 on Apr 12 (Dan Patrick warning about Talarico's Senate chances), then rapidly decayed: 100→38→6. Baseline was 0 for 17 consecutive days before the spike.

2. **Current value of 6 is above the ±3 threshold floor.** Apr 25 just needs to be < 3 (i.e., 0-2) for "Decreases." With baseline at 0 and rapid decay, this is the most likely outcome.

3. **SerpAPI measurement uncertainty is the main swing factor.** If SerpAPI reports Apr 14 ≤ 3 instead of pytrends' 6, then even Apr 25 = 0 would resolve as "Doesn't change."

4. **New spike unlikely for this exact phrase.** While the Texas Senate race is active, "patrick warns talarico could win" is tied to one specific news cycle. New developments would use different search terms.

resolution_criteria: This question resolves “Doesn't change” if the value on the timeline at [https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=patrick%20warns%20talarico%20could%20win&date=2026-03-26%202026-04-25](https://trends.google.com/trends/explore?geo=US&tz=0&q=patrick%20warns%20talarico%20could%20win&date=2026-03-26%202026-04-25) for 2026-04-25 is within 3 of the value at 2026-04-14. It resolves “Increases” if the value is more than 3 greater, and resolves “Decreases” if the value is more than 3 lower.
fine_print:
A script will be used to determine the resolution of this question. It will access the data on Google Trends using [SerpApi](https://serpapi.com/), and compare the height of the timeline for the two aforementioned dates. The specific python query will be structured as follows:
```
from serpapi import GoogleSearch

search = GoogleSearch(params={
  "api_key": API_KEY,
  "engine": "google_trends",
  "data_type": "TIMESERIES",
  "geo": "US",
  "tz": 0,
  "q": "patrick warns talarico could win",
  "date": "2026-03-26 2026-04-25",
})
search.get_dict()
```
Note that there may be differences between the results returned by the API and the data appearing on the page. This seems to be due to the 'tz' parameter not having the intended effect in-browser. In this case, the API results will be considered authoritative.
Note that the precision of the timeline will be 1 day, so this will compare the overall interest for the whole day as determined by Google Trends.
Dates are determined in UTC.
If the data is no longer available, or the script fails, this question will be annulled or manually resolved by a moderator.
